title: (No AI) Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text

title: (No AI) AWS boss explains why investing billions in both Anthropic and OpenAI is an OK conflict

title: (No AI) Gemini gets notebooks to help you organize projects
author: Jay Peters
content:
![Illustration of the Google Gemini logo.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/02/STK255_Google_Gemini_A.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Google's Gemini is getting a feature called "notebooks" to help you organize things about certain topics in a single place while using the AI chatbot, [the company announced on Wednesday](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/products/gemini-app/notebooks-gemini-notebooklm/). You can pull in things like files, past conversations, and custom instructions into notebooks that Gemini can then use as context while you're talking with it.

Notebooks sound a lot like ChatGPT's Projects feature, which launched [in 2024](https://www.theverge.com/2024/12/13/24320800/openai-chatgpt-projects-folders-ai-chats) and similarly [lets users](https://help.openai.com/en/articles/10169521-projects-in-chatgpt) store things about a certain topic in one spot. Google says to "think of notebooks as personal knowledge bases shared across Google products, starting in Gemini." Gemini's Notebooks also sync with …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/909031/google-gemini-notebooks-notebooklm)
contentSnippet:
Google's Gemini is getting a feature called "notebooks" to help you organize things about certain topics in a single place while using the AI chatbot, the company announced on Wednesday. You can pull in things like files, past conversations, and custom instructions into notebooks that Gemini can then use as context while you're talking with it. 
Notebooks sound a lot like ChatGPT's Projects feature, which launched in 2024 and similarly lets users store things about a certain topic in one spot. Google says to "think of notebooks as personal knowledge bases shared across Google products, starting in Gemini." Gemini's Notebooks also sync with  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

You can pull in things like files, past conversations, and custom instructions into notebooks that Gemini can then use as context while you're talking with […]

title: (No AI) OpenAI made economic proposals — here’s what DC thinks of them
author: Tina Nguyen
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/STK155_OPEN_AI_4_CVirginia_D.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

[_Happy ceasefire day_](https://www.yahoo.com/news/world/article/trump-agrees-to-two-week-ceasefire-deal-with-iran-after-threatening-to-wipe-out-its-whole-civilization-how-we-got-here-and-what-could-happen-next-193210369.html) _and welcome to_ Regulator_, a newsletter for_ Verge _subscribers about Big Tech's rocky journey through the world of politics. If you're not a subscriber yet,_ [_you can do so here_](https://www.theverge.com/subscribe)_, but my only request is that you sign up before Donald Trump decides to revisit his previous threats toward Iran and kickstart World War III._

  
_I'm back after being waylaid last week by the deadly combo of a moderate cold and the beginning of pollen season. (Twenty-one percent of the District's acreage is taken up by public green space, and DC is consistently ranked_ [_the best city park system in America_](https://www.tpl.org/media-room/trust-for-public-land-names-washington-dc-best-big-city-park-system-washington-defends-parkscore-title-as-irvine-soars-to-runner-up-finish)_. Unfortunately, I am allergic to every tree and gr …_

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/column/908880/openai-made-economic-proposals-heres-what-dc-thinks-of-them)

creator: Paresh Dave
title: (No AI) Conflicting Rulings Leave Anthropic in ‘Supply-Chain Risk’ Limbo
dc:creator: Paresh Dave
content: A US appeals court ruling is at odds with a separate, lower court decision from March, leaving uncertainty about if and how the US military can use the AI company's Claude model.
contentSnippet: A US appeals court ruling is at odds with a separate, lower court decision from March, leaving uncertainty about if and how the US military can use the AI company's Claude model.
